system_prompt:
# Baseline Agent

You are a customer service agent. Your goal is to help the user complete their request efficiently and accurately.

## Instructions

- Identify what the user needs and take action to resolve it.
- Use the available tools to look up information and perform actions.
- Be helpful, clear, and professional.
- If you cannot help with a request, transfer to a human agent.
- Before making any changes (cancellations, modifications, exchanges, returns), list the details and ask the user to confirm.

messages.content: Hi, this is Emma Kim. I need to cancel my reservation—confirmation number EHGLP3. I know it might be more than 24 hours since I booked, but I was out of town and couldn’t call earlier. Can you help me with the cancellation? Also, I’d like to know if I’ll get a refund before I proceed.

I am able to assist with your cancellation request, but I need to confirm a few details before proceeding:

1. The reason for cancellation (for example: change of plan, airline cancelled flight, or other reasons).
2. Whether any portion of your flight has already been flown.

Once I have this information, I can check if you are eligible for a refund and proceed with the cancellation. Could you please provide the reason for cancellation and confirm if any part of your trip has already been flown?

Sure, thank you. The reason for cancellation is a change of plans—I won’t be able to travel anymore. None of the flights have been flown yet; the trip hasn’t started. 

Can you let me know if I’m eligible for a refund before you cancel? I really don’t want to cancel unless I’ll get my money back.

To determine your refund eligibility, I need to check a few details about your reservation, such as the cabin class, booking date, and whether you have travel insurance. 

Could you please confirm:
- The cabin class (basic economy, economy, or business)?
- If you purchased travel insurance with your reservation?
- Approximately when you booked the reservation (if you remember)?

This information will help me accurately assess your refund eligibility before proceeding with cancellation.

I booked an economy class ticket, and I don’t think I purchased travel insurance with this reservation. I booked it a few days ago, but I’m not sure of the exact date—maybe three or four days back.

When I booked my previous trip through your agency, I had insurance, but this time I was told I didn’t need it. That’s why I didn’t add it. Does that affect my refund eligibility? I just want to make sure I won’t lose my money if I cancel.

Thank you for the details. Here’s how refund eligibility works for your situation:

- Since you booked an economy class ticket and did not purchase travel insurance, your eligibility for a refund depends on the following:
  - If your booking was made within the last 24 hours, you are eligible for a full refund.
  - If the airline cancelled your flight, you are eligible for a refund.
  - If you booked a business class ticket, you would be eligible for a refund at any time before the flight.
  - If you have travel insurance and the cancellation reason is covered, you are eligible for a refund.

Based on what you shared:
- Your booking was made more than 24 hours ago.
- The reason for cancellation is a change of plans (not airline cancellation).
- You do not have travel insurance.
- Your ticket is economy class.

**In this case, you are not eligible for a refund if you cancel your reservation.** Cancelling now would mean you would not get your money back.

Would you like to keep your reservation, or do you want to proceed with the cancellation knowing that it will be non-refundable? Please let me know how you’d like to proceed.

Oh, I see. I was actually told when I booked that I didn’t need to get insurance, since my previous trip with your agency had insurance and everything was fine. If I had known I wouldn’t get a refund, I definitely would have added insurance this time. Is there any way you can make an exception, since I was relying on the information I got from your team? I really don’t want to lose all my money.

If there’s absolutely no way to get a refund, then I’d rather keep the reservation for now. But if there’s anything you can do, I’d really appreciate it.

I understand your situation and your concern about the refund. Unfortunately, based on the airline policy, I am not able to make exceptions for refund eligibility if travel insurance was not purchased and the booking is for economy class with a cancellation due to change of plans.

If you would like to discuss your case further or request an exception, I can transfer you to a human agent who may be able to review your situation in more detail.

Would you like me to transfer you to a human agent for further assistance, or would you prefer to keep your reservation as it is? Please let me know how you’d like to proceed.
Yes, please transfer me to a human agent. I’d really like to discuss this further and see if there’s anything that can be done. Thank you for your help so far.

arguments.summary: Emma Kim wants to cancel her economy class reservation (EHGLP3) due to a change of plans. She did not purchase travel insurance and booked more than 24 hours ago. She is requesting an exception for a refund, as she relied on previous information from the agency. Please review her case for possible refund or compensation.

messages.content:
YOU ARE BEING TRANSFERRED TO A HUMAN AGENT. PLEASE HOLD ON.

A human agent will review your situation and discuss possible options with you. Thank you for your patience.
